name: EMRS — Eklavya Model Residential School
summary: Ministry of Tribal Affairs government residential schools for ST (Scheduled Tribe) students — completely free education, boarding, food, and books from Class 6 to 12, on par with Navodaya Vidyalayas, with focus on tribal districts.
amount_inr: Free — tuition, boarding, meals, books, and uniform all covered
deadline_pattern: Admission via EMRSST (a selection test administered in Odisha by OMTES). Entry primarily at Class 6; lateral entry to Classes 7–9 for vacancies. Admission guidelines published yearly by OMTES — verify at omtes.org or emrs.tribal.gov.in.
documents: Birth certificate or age proof (2-year age relaxation for differently-abled)
Domicile / residence certificate (for the notified tribal district)
notes: Odisha numbers: 114 EMRS sanctioned, approximately 47 functional (serving 13,000+ tribal students), 64 under construction, 3 at land-identification stage — as of OMTES data. Some third-party sources cite '104' — treat OMTES figures as authoritative and cite 'as of December 2025.' Notable districts: Mayurbhanj, Malkangiri, Nabarangpur, Rayagada. ST category is mandatory. The selection test (EMRSST) is administered by OMTES — contact omtes.org for the current cycle schedule.

name: KISS — Kalinga Institute of Social Sciences, Bhubaneswar
odia_name: KISS — କଳିଙ୍ଗ ସାମାଜିକ ବିଜ୍ଞାନ ଅନୁଷ୍ଠାନ, ଭୁବନେଶ୍ୱର
hindi_name: KISS — कलिंग इंस्टीट्यूट ऑफ सोशल साइंसेज, भुवनेश्वर
summary: World's largest fully free residential institution for ST (tribal / Adivasi) students — from KG to university graduation; everything is free: tuition, boarding, food, healthcare, clothing, and vocational training; deemed university since 2017.
amount_inr: Free — education, boarding, food, healthcare, clothing, and vocational training all covered
deadline_pattern: Admissions open periodically; the most recent notice was for ST students for Classes 1–5 (with ~60% preference for girls). BPL condition for out-of-state children. Verify at kiss.ac.in — do not rely on third-party dates.
documents: BPL card or income certificate (required for out-of-state children)
notes: Founded 1993 by Prof. Achyuta Samanta. Total enrollment ~27,000 current students; 40,000+ including alumni (figures cited at different times — use 'approximately' and cite year). KALSEE (Kalinga Social Science Entrance Examination) is referenced for higher-level entry. Located in Bhubaneswar (Kalinga Nagar campus). About 60% of students are girls. For students outside Odisha: BPL condition applies. Higher classes: verify KALSEE or direct admission process at kiss.ac.in.

name: Anwesha — English-Medium Schooling for Disadvantaged ST / SC Children
odia_name: ଅନ୍ୱେଷା — ST/SC ପିଲାଙ୍କ ଇଂଲିଶ ମାଧ୍ୟମ ଶିକ୍ଷା
hindi_name: अन्वेषा — ST/SC बच्चों के लिए अंग्रेजी माध्यम शिक्षा
summary: Odisha ST & SC Development Department scheme placing disadvantaged ST/SC children from 17 tribal districts in urban English-medium schools with hostels — fully funded; covers tuition, uniform, books, transport, boarding, and after-school tutoring. Entry at Class 1.
amount_inr: Free — tuition, uniform, books, transport, boarding, and after-school tutoring all covered by the state
deadline_pattern: Selection by lottery; entry primarily at Class 1. Contact your district ST & SC Development Office or check stsc.odisha.gov.in for the current cycle. Application typically before Class 1 admission season (March–May).
documents: BPL card or income/poverty certificate (remote area / BPL focus)
Domicile certificate from one of the 17 tribal-concentrated districts
notes: Scale (figures vary by source and year): approximately 21,000–22,340 children enrolled across approximately 80–98 hostels and 135–177 partner schools. Launched 21 December 2015; extended in 2020 for 5 more years (₹569.90 crore). Covers 17 tribal-concentrated districts. English-medium instruction gives these children the same advantage as urban private-school students. IMPORTANT: Entry is at Class 1 — if a student is in Class 9–12 already, this scheme likely does not apply. Direct Class 9+ students to EMRS or scholarship options instead.

name: KMRS — Kalinga Model Residential School (Odisha ST & SC Dept / OMTES)
summary: Additional Odisha residential school option for tribal and SC students, run alongside EMRS under OMTES / the ST & SC Development Department — free residential education for disadvantaged students.
deadline_pattern: Verify current admission process and calendar at omtes.org or stsc.odisha.gov.in.
notes: Less documented in available sources than EMRS and Anwesha — verify current number of functional schools, intake classes, and eligibility directly with OMTES or the district ST & SC office.

name: BPAV — Biju Patnaik Adarsh Vidyalaya
summary: Odisha residential school model named after Biju Patnaik — another government residential option for disadvantaged students, referenced alongside EMRS and KMRS in Odisha's tribal education system.
deadline_pattern: Verify at omtes.org or stsc.odisha.gov.in for current admissions.
notes: Limited documentation in current sources — verify current scope, eligibility, and whether BPAV is open to non-tribal students, with OMTES or the local district education office.
